rulings.Case Title: ADAM SMITH ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 23, 2022 line 1. RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is continued to March 30, 2022 per stipulation and order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DONALD A. PETERSON VS. JEAN I. MENES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Peterson V Menes - Notice And Motion For Court Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 23, 2022 line 3. PLAINTIFF DONALD PETERSON'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO FILE AND RECORD SUPERSEDING LIS PENDENS is GRANTED. Lis Pendens in the proper form with notice provided to all entitled to such notice shall be recorded, filed and served forthwith. Plaintiff seeks permission per CCP 405.36 to record a lis pendens after expungement. Both times the Court expunged the Lis Pendens/Errata on procedural grounds (per CCP 405.20 and for failure to comply with CCP 405.36) and stayed this partition action which cannot proceed in the absence of a valid lis pendens. Plaintiff now moves to record a lis pendens as required by CCP 872.250. The nature of the case is described in the moving papers, CCP 872.250 is referenced in the moving papers, relevant documents, including orders, are attached to the moving papers. Defendant's reliance on factually and procedurally distinguishable Ranchito Ownership v. Sup. Ct. (1982) 130 Cal.App.3d 764 is misplaced. The Court notes that Plaintiff's lack of attention to detail creates ambiguity and urges Plaintiff's counsel to proofread papers intended for filing/recordation. See discrepancy in the hearing date on the Notice and Declaration; reference to a wrong action within October 14, 2020 filing. The Court further notes that its decision is not based on the sections of the reply that were the subject to Defendant's March 21, 2022 ex-parte application. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JERRY LEE VS. SALOMON RIZZO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 23, 2022 line 4. DEFENDANT SALOMON RIZZO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint asserts a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is sufficiently clear for Defendant to respond, i.e. is not uncertain withing the meaning of CCP 430.10(f). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
